THOUGHT:
The search results successfully returned the exact paper needed to cover the data leakage pitfall. Paper 0 is the peer-reviewed publication in *Patterns* (2023) by Kapoor and Narayanan, which systematically investigates data leakage as a major driver of reproducibility failures in ML. It provides a comprehensive taxonomy of eight leakage types and empirical evidence of how leakage drastically inflates validation metrics. This perfectly aligns with the missing citation category. I will select this paper to discuss specific leakage mechanisms, compare flawed validation practices with robust out-of-sample evaluation, highlight gaps in current data partitioning protocols, and suggest future research directions for automated leakage detection and standardized reporting.
